findings.evidence: PrimeRadicands.lean:73-75 `refine not_isSquare_prod_primes p (fun i _ => hp i) ?_ hS; intro i _ j _ hij h; exact hij (hinj h)` vs PrimeGaloisGroup.lean:40-45 same body.
findings.fix: Add a shared injective-form lemma in PrimeRadicands.lean (e.g. `not_isSquare_prod_primes` taking `Function.Injective p`) and call it from both `finrank_adjoin_sqrt_primes` and this file.
findings.issue: Private `not_isSquare_prod_sqrt_primes` re-derives the injective→pairwise adaptation of `not_isSquare_prod_primes` already written inline at PrimeRadicands.lean:73-75 (which this file imports), duplicating it across both prime corollaries.

findings.evidence: PrimeRadicands.lean:72 and PrimeGaloisGroup.lean:37 are identical proof terms.
findings.fix: Extract this one-liner into a shared helper in PrimeRadicands.lean and reuse it in both `finrank_adjoin_sqrt_primes` and `galoisGroupEquivSqrtPrimes`/`card_aut_adjoin_sqrt_primes`.
findings.issue: Private `sqrt_nat_sq` repeats the radicand-square step `rw [Real.sq_sqrt (Nat.cast_nonneg _), map_natCast]` already present inline at PrimeRadicands.lean:72.

summary: New public declarations are genuinely new and reuse galoisGroupEquiv/card_aut_adjoin_range/not_isSquare_prod_primes correctly. But the two private helpers re-derive logic already inlined in the imported PrimeRadicands.lean; factor those out once and share.
